articles.full_text:
Hyderabad Weather Alert: Temperature Soars To 40°C, Residents Told To Stay Indoors
Telangana faces early summer heat with temperatures reaching 36-40°C. Authorities advise hydration, sun protection, and caution for vulnerable groups over the coming days.
Telangana is experiencing the summer heat unusually early this year, with temperatures soaring between 36°C and 40°C across several districts. The scorching midday sun is making outdoor activities increasingly difficult, and authorities have urged residents to take precautionary measures to stay safe.
Typically, extreme summer conditions peak in April, but this year, the heat has arrived as early as March, catching many by surprise. The Hyderabad Meteorological Centre has attributed the high temperatures to two upper-level atmospheric ridges.
One ridge extends from the Gulf of Mannar, across Tamil Nadu and interior Karnataka, while the other stretches from northern Odisha through southern Chhattisgarh to Vidarbha. These atmospheric systems are bringing strong, hot winds across the state, intensifying the heat and making outdoor conditions uncomfortable for residents.
Over the next three days, dry weather is expected to dominate the region, with no rainfall predicted. Meteorologists anticipate that the same weather pattern will continue through Wednesday and Thursday, and some areas could see maximum temperatures climb by an additional 2-3°C.
Districts such as Adilabad, Kothagudem, Gadwal, Asifabad, Mancherial, Mulugu, Narayanpet, Nirmal, and Nizamabad are likely to experience the highest intensity of heat, with the midday sun particularly harsh in these areas. Health officials have issued several precautions to help residents cope with the early summer conditions.
Citizens are advised to stay well-hydrated by drinking plenty of water, avoiding prolonged exposure to direct sunlight, wearing protective headgear such as caps or hats, and opting for lightweight, breathable clothing. Vulnerable groups, including elderly citizens, children, and individuals with chronic health conditions, are urged to take extra care and avoid unnecessary outdoor activities during peak hours.
With summer arriving unusually early in Telangana, experts stress the importance of following safety measures to prevent heat-related illnesses such as dehydration, heat exhaustion, or heatstroke. By taking simple precautions and remaining aware of weather updates, residents can protect themselves while navigating the rising temperatures in the coming days.
